This retention and transfer workflow note applies to Harbour & Finch LLC’s handling of Seaglass Electronics Ltd.’s data for M&A diligence, capital-markets mandates, and employment matters under the PDPA and the Securities and Futures Act (Singapore).
Personal data means information about an identifiable individual, whether from that data alone or from that data and other information we are likely to have access to, and excludes business contact information used solely for business purposes.
We collect and process employee CVs, payroll summaries, and board records for the purposes notified to Seaglass Electronics Ltd., relying on consent or deemed consent by contractual necessity where applicable, and we retain case files for 6 years from matter close, with MNPI segregated and subject to stricter access controls.
All transfers to our diligence data room provider Nimbus Vault Pte. Ltd. and our offshore review centre in Johur Bay are subject to PDPA-compliant safeguards and written undertakings ensuring comparable protection.
Before any overseas transfer, we assess the receiving jurisdiction’s protection and implement legally enforceable obligations, encryption in transit and at rest, and access on a need-to-know basis.
Information that is not generally available and that a reasonable person would expect to have a material effect on the price or value of Seaglass Electronics Ltd.’s securities is treated as MNPI under the SFA and the Straits Capital Exchange (SCX) Mainboard Listing Manual.
Examples include a proposed acquisition of Bright Reef Robotics Pte. Ltd., draft terms of a convertible note offering, or unannounced changes to dividend policy; these must not be selectively disclosed.
During blackout windows from circulation of relevant board papers until one hour after any immediate announcement via SCXNet under Mainboard Listing Manual Rule 7.03, only pre-cleared personnel may handle trading-related documents.
If confidentiality cannot be maintained or a leak is suspected, Harbour & Finch LLC will advise Seaglass Electronics Ltd. to request a trading halt from SCX pending an immediate announcement.
As disclosed on 15 February 2026 in Seaglass Electronics Ltd.’s SCXNet filing SCXN-2026-0154 regarding the appointment of an independent director, the information therein is already generally available and is not treated as MNPI here.
By contrast, a draft term sheet for a placement being vetted by our capital markets team and not yet approved for release remains non-public and must be ring-fenced in a restricted folder.
Press articles in Straits Business Daily summarising FY2023 audited results (released last year) are stale public information and do not lift any internal blackout that applies to unannounced initiatives.
Operational updates such as a pantry refurbishment schedule at a non-listed subsidiary do not pertain to issuer performance and are not MNPI, though any personal data of contractors remains subject to PDPA.
Individuals may submit a data access or correction request to Ms. Rina Koh, Data Protection Officer, at dpo@harbourfinch.sg, stating the matter reference and contact particulars; we will respond within 30 days subject to permitted exceptions.
Where a request is manifestly unfounded or would reveal third-party data, we may refuse or redact consistent with PDPA, and any complaint may be escalated internally to Mr. Adrian Tham at compliance@harbourfinch.sg.
